saṇṭhapemi.meaning: I establish; I straighten, settle (the mind)
saṇṭhapemi.gram: verb, present tense 1st person singular (causative of saṇṭhahati, √ṭhā)
breakdown: Root “to stand”; causative: to set up, establish.

sedāvakkhittā.meaning: gained by sweat; earned by the sweat (of one's brow)
breakdown: Thrown down, poured out (past participle of avakkhipati). Together: won by pouring out sweat.

sekhasuttaṁ.meaning: the Sekha Sutta, the Discourse on the Trainee
sekhasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a discourse)
breakdown: Trainee, learner — one still in training on the path.

sekhena.meaning: by one still in training (not yet an arahant)
sekhena.gram: masculine agent noun (instrumental singular), from sikkhati

sekho.meaning: one in training; a learner not yet perfected
sekho.summary: Technical term for a noble disciple at any of the first seven stages, still short of arahantship.
sekho.gram: masculine agent noun (nominative singular), from sikkhati

setaparivāro.meaning: with a white retinue; having a following clothed in white

setughāto.meaning: the destruction of the bridge (the complete cutting-off of the causes of wrongdoing)

sevitabbasuttaṁ.meaning: the Sevitabba Sutta, the Discourse on What Should Be Associated With
sevitabbasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a discourse)
breakdown: To be associated with, to be pursued (potential participle of sevati).

sevitabbo.meaning: should be associated with; to be followed, resorted to
sevitabbo.gram: potential participle of sevati (nominative singular masculine)
breakdown: Root meaning “to serve, associate with, practise”.

sevitabbā.meaning: should be pursued; to be practised, cultivated
sevitabbā.gram: potential participle of sevati (nominative plural / feminine singular)

seyyathidaṁ.meaning: namely; that is to say; as follows

seyyāvasathapadīpeyyassa.meaning: of bedding, dwelling and lamp-materials (gifts of bed, lodging and lighting)

sikkhāsājīvasamāpanno.meaning: having entered upon the training and way of life (of the monks)
breakdown: Way of life, the rule of livelihood shared by the community.
Entered upon, undertaken (past participle of samāpajjati).

sippapaṭiggahaṇena.meaning: by the learning of a craft; through receiving (instruction in) a skill
breakdown: Accepting, receiving (paṭi + √gah). (The doubled gg is sandhi doubling.)

sippaññatarena.meaning: by one or another craft; by some skill or other
breakdown: One or another, a certain. (The -a + a- gives -añña- in sandhi.)

sito.meaning: fixed; planted; attached to, dependent on
sito.gram: past participle (nominative singular masculine), from sayati/sita

siyā.meaning: should be; may be; could be
siyā.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular of atthi (√as), irregular

siṅghāṭakena.meaning: by the crossroads; at the place where four roads meet
breakdown: Crossroads, a square where four roads meet.

socanaṭṭhāneyeva.meaning: just in the place of grieving; precisely where there is occasion for sorrow
socanaṭṭhāneyeva.gram: neuter noun, compound (locative singular) + emphatic yeva
breakdown: Place, occasion. (The doubled ṭṭh is sandhi doubling.)

socare.meaning: they grieve (middle voice; in context with na: they do not grieve)
socare.gram: verb, present middle 3rd person plural of socati

breakdown: Abstract noun suffix: the state of purity.
soceyya.note: divergence: DPD's parser links the form to socati “to grieve”; the lexicon's gloss “purity” (from suci) fits the attested context and is followed.

socitattaṁ.meaning: sorrowfulness; the state of being grieved

sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsa.meaning: sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief and despair
sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsa.summary: The standard fivefold list of mental and bodily suffering in the formula of dukkha.
sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsa.gram: masculine compound noun (stem form; plural sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsā)
sokaparidevadukkhadomanassupāyāsa.goenka: This fivefold chain — sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief and despair — recurs in the exposition of the Noble Truth of Suffering and in the formula of dependent origination: with birth as condition, “sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief and despair” arise. In the satipaṭṭhāna teaching the meditator contemplates precisely these states as they manifest, with awareness and equanimity, so that the whole mass of suffering may be brought to an end.

sopapajjati.meaning: he arises; he is reborn (so + upapajjati)
sopapajjati.gram: sandhi fusion: pronoun + verb, present tense 3rd person singular
breakdown: He (nominative singular pronoun, referring back to naro “the man”).
Arises, is reborn (upa + √pad). (The o + u- coalesce in sandhi.)
Root meaning “to hear” (savati/suṇāti); sota as “stream” derives from √su “to flow”.

sotabbaṁ.meaning: should be heard; worth listening to
sotabbaṁ.gram: potential participle of suṇāti (nominative/accusative singular neuter)

sotaṁ odahatha.meaning: lend your ear!; you should listen (literally: apply the ear)
sotaṁ odahatha.gram: phrase: noun (accusative singular) + verb (imperative 2nd person plural)
breakdown: Apply!, place down! (o/ava + √dhā, imperative 2nd person plural).

sotāpattiphalaṁ.meaning: the fruit of stream-entry; the fruition of entering the stream
breakdown: Stream (the stream of the Dhamma leading to liberation).
sotāpattiphalaṁ.goenka: Sotāpattiphala is the fruit of entering the stream — the first of the four stages of liberation. The sotāpanna has experienced nibbāna for the first time, the illusion of self is shattered, and final liberation within at most seven lives is assured: one has entered the stream that flows irreversibly to full freedom.

sovacassatā.meaning: amenability to instruction; being easy to admonish; obedience
breakdown: Root meaning “to speak”. Sovacassa: being easy to speak to, receptive to correction.
sovacassatā.goenka: Sovacassatā, being easy to instruct, is praised in the Maṅgalasutta among the highest welfares. The meditator who receives admonition with humility rather than resentment keeps the door open for growth on the path; surrender to the discipline of the teaching is itself a protection.

sovaggikaṁ.meaning: leading to heaven; connected with the heavenly fields
breakdown: Adjectival suffix: relating to, conducive to.

soṇḍiṁ.meaning: a drunkard (feminine); a woman addicted to drink

subhaddo vuḍḍhapabbajito..meaning: Subhadda, the one gone forth in old age
subhaddo vuḍḍhapabbajito..gram: phrase: proper noun + adjective compound (nominative singular)
subhaddo vuḍḍhapabbajito..goenka: The commentary relates that at one time Subhadda, who had already gone forth, invited the Buddha and his disciples to a meal. But because he had collected the stock for his offering by sending his two sons, then novices, around the village of Ātumā to gather what they could, the Buddha refused the invitation, saying that Subhadda had committed a wrongdoing by causing others to give at no cost to himself.

subhāsita.goenka: Subhāsita, well-spoken speech, stands opposed to deliberate false speech. The lexicon recalls the Jayamaṅgala-aṭṭhagāthā account of Ciñcā, an attractive woman who falsely accused the Buddha — a notable example of one who, despite better knowledge, deliberately deviates from the truth. Such a demeritorious act of speech, sampajānamusāvāda (intentional and deliberate lying), is highlighted in the Musāvādasutta; cultivating well-spoken, truthful words is the positive side of right speech.

subhāsitadubbhāsitassa.meaning: of what is well spoken and badly spoken
breakdown: Prefix: badly, ill. (The doubled bb is sandhi doubling.)

subhāvitaṁ.meaning: well developed; well cultivated (of the mind, of mettā)
breakdown: Developed, cultivated (past participle of bhāveti, causative of √bhū “to be”).
